heading: Push Live Migration CLI Commands
body:
To migrate a cluster using the Atlas CLI, you can perform the following steps:

- Create or delete a link-token

- Create or view a validation job

- Create or view a migration job

- Perform the cutover

For other steps in the live migration procedure, you must use the Cloud Manager UI or the Atlas UI.

Before you migrate a cluster using the Atlas CLI, complete the pre-migration validation.

> **Note:** Before you run any Atlas CLI commands, you must:
>
> - [Install the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/install-atlas-cli)
>
> - [Connect to the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/connect-atlas-cli)

To create a new [link-token](manual/reference/glossary) using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas liveMigrations link create [options]

```

To delete the [link-token](manual/reference/glossary) you specify using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas liveMigrations link delete [options]

```

To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas liveMigrations link create](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-liveMigrations-link-create) and [atlas liveMigrations link delete](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-liveMigrations-link-delete).

If you are migrating from Ops Manager, request an external IP address
and specify it in the link-token. To learn more, see
[Request an External IP Address](ops-manager/tutorial/migrate-to-atlas) in the
Ops Manager documentation.

To create a new validation request using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas liveMigrations validation create [options]

```

To return the details for the validation request you specify using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas liveMigrations validation describe [options]

```

To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas liveMigrations validation create](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-liveMigrations-validation-create) and [atlas liveMigrations validation describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-liveMigrations-validation-describe).

To learn what Atlas validates, see the Validate bullet
in the Migrate Your Cluster section on this page.

To create one new migration job using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas liveMigrations create [options]

```

To return the details of the migration job you specify using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas liveMigrations describe [options]

```

To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas liveMigrations create](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-liveMigrations-create) and [atlas liveMigrations describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-liveMigrations-describe).

To start the cutover for live migration using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas liveMigrations cutover [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas liveMigrations cutover](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-liveMigrations-cutover).

When the cutover completes, Atlas completes the live migration process and stops synchronizing with the source cluster. To learn
more, see the Migrate Your Cluster section on this page.

> **Note:** The Live Migration CLI commands might mention Cloud Manager or Ops Manager, however, the
> type of live migration described in this section only supports
> migrating source clusters monitored in Cloud Manager to destination
> clusters in Atlas.
